title: New Chaos Variant Targets Misconfigured Cloud Deployments, Adds SOCKS Proxy
summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new variant ofmalware called Chaosthat'scapable of hitting misconfigured cloud deployments, marking an expansion of the botnet's targeting infrastructure.

sections.overview: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new variant ofmalware called Chaosthat'scapable of hitting misconfigured cloud deployments, marking an expansion of the botnet's targeting infrastructure.
sections.technical:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new variant ofmalware called Chaosthat'scapable of hitting misconfigured cloud deployments, marking an expansion of the botnet's targeting infrastructure.
"Chaos malware is increasingly targeting misconfigured cloud deployments, expanding beyond its traditional focus on routers and edge devices," Darktrace said in a new report.

title: Masjesu Botnet Emerges as DDoS-for-Hire Service Targeting Global IoT Devices
summary: Cybersecurity researchers have lifted the curtain on a stealthy botnet that's designed for distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks.

sections.overview:
Cybersecurity researchers have lifted the curtain on a stealthy botnet that's designed for distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks. Called Masjesu, the botnet has been advertised via Telegram as a DDoS-for-hire service since it first surfaced in 2023. It's capable of targeting a wide range of IoT devices, such as routers and gateways, spanning multiple architectures.
"Built for

title: Threat Actors Get Crafty With Emojis to Escape Detection

title: AI-Led Remediation Crisis Prompts HackerOne to Pause Bug Bounties
summary: Discovery used to be the bottleneck for open source bugs, but with automated discovery, remediation's the bottleneck, which bounties don't fund.

title: APT28 Deploys PRISMEX Malware in Campaign Targeting Ukraine and NATO Allies
summary: The Russian threat actor known as APT28 (aka Forest Blizzard and Pawn Storm) has been linked to a fresh spear-phishing campaign targeting Ukraine and its allies to deploy a previously undocumented malware suite codenamed PRISMEX.

sections.overview: The Russian threat actor known as APT28 (aka Forest Blizzard and Pawn Storm) has been linked to a fresh spear-phishing campaign targeting Ukraine and its allies to deploy a previously undocumented malware suite codenamed PRISMEX.
sections.technical:
The Russian threat actor known as APT28 (aka Forest Blizzard and Pawn Storm) has been linked to a fresh spear-phishing campaign targeting Ukraine and its allies to deploy a previously undocumented malware suite codenamed PRISMEX.
"PRISMEX combines advanced steganography, component object model (COM) hijacking, and legitimate cloud service abuse for command-and-control," Trend Micro
sections.impact:

sections.mitigation: Reinforce security awareness training. Ensure email filtering, link scanning, and multi-factor authentication are enabled. Review endpoint detection rules and update threat intelligence feeds. Scan for known indicators of compromise.
sections.context:
Advanced persistent threat groups typically conduct long-running, targeted campaigns often aligned with nation-state interests.

Phishing remains the most common initial access vector. Security awareness training combined with technical controls like email filtering and MFA are key defenses.

Modern malware campaigns frequently use multi-stage delivery chains and evasion techniques. Defense-in-depth strategies including EDR, network monitoring, and application allowlisting help reduce exposure.

title: Shrinking the IAM Attack Surface through Identity Visibility and Intelligence Platforms (IVIP)
summary: The Fragmented State of Modern Enterprise Identity

sections.overview:
The Fragmented State of Modern Enterprise Identity

title: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Finds Thousands of Zero-Day Flaws Across Major Systems

title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
summary: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation.

sections.overview:
CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-1340 Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) Code Injection Vulnerability

 
 This type of vulnerability is a frequent attack vector for malicious cyber actors and poses significant risks to the federal enterprise. Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 22-01: Reducing the Significant Risk of Known Exploited Vulnerabilities established the KEV Catalog as a living list of known Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) that carry significant risk to the federal enterprise.
sections.technical:
CVE-2026-1340 Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) Code Injection Vulnerability

 
 This type of vulnerability is a frequent attack vector for malicious cyber actors and poses significant risks to the federal enterprise. CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 22-01: Reducing the Significant Risk of Known Exploited Vulnerabilities established the KEV Catalog as a living list of known Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) that carry significant risk to the federal enterprise. Although BOD 22-01 only applies to FCEB agencies, CISA strongly urges all organizations to reduce their exposure to cyberattacks by prioritizing timely remediation of KEV Catalog vulnerabilities as part of their vulnerability management practice.

Referenced vulnerabilities: CVE-2026-1340.
sections.impact:
CVE-2026-1340 Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) Code Injection Vulnerability

 
 This type of vulnerability is a frequent attack vector for malicious cyber actors and poses significant risks to the federal enterprise. Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 22-01: Reducing the Significant Risk of Known Exploited Vulnerabilities established the KEV Catalog as a living list of known Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) that carry significant risk to the federal enterprise. Although BOD 22-01 only applies to FCEB agencies, CISA strongly urges all organizations to reduce their exposure to cyberattacks by prioritizing timely remediation of KEV Catalog vulnerabilities as part of their vulnerability management practice.
sections.mitigation:
BOD 22-01 requires Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to remediate identified vulnerabilities by the due date to protect FCEB networks against active threats.

Apply vendor-supplied patches as soon as possible. If patching is not immediately feasible, implement recommended workarounds and compensating controls. Review endpoint detection rules and update threat intelligence feeds. Scan for known indicators of compromise.
sections.context:
Timely vulnerability management and patch prioritization remain critical components of any defensive security program.

Modern malware campaigns frequently use multi-stage delivery chains and evasion techniques. Defense-in-depth strategies including EDR, network monitoring, and application allowlisting help reduce exposure.

email_summary: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. Tracked as CVE-2026-1340.

title: Iran-Linked Hackers Disrupt U.S. Critical Infrastructure by Targeting Internet-Exposed PLCs
